mission.title: Install and Configure Kaito on Kubernetes
mission.description: KAITO is a Kubernetes AI Toolchain Operator that automates the AI/ML model inference and tuning workloads in a Kubernetes cluster. It simplifies the deployment of large AI inference models by managing GPU resources and providing preset configurations for model requirements.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KAITO Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kaito https://kaito-project.github.io/kaito/charts/kaito
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Update your Helm repositories to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KAITO using Helm
steps.description:
Install the KAITO operator with the following command, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist:
```bash
helm install kaito gpu-provisioner --namespace kaito --create-namespace --version 0.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the KAITO pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kaito
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the KAITO operator by editing the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kaito --namespace kaito
``` 
Add resource limits under the `spec.template.spec.containers` section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KAITO operator with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall kaito --namespace kaito
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
uninstall.description:
Remove the CRDs associated with KAITO:
```bash
kubectl delete crd workspaces.kaito.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for KAITO:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kaito
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configuration and resources:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kaito -o yaml > kaito-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update Helm repositories
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repositories are up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KAITO
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the KAITO operator to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade kaito gpu-provisioner --namespace kaito --version 0.2.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the KAITO pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kaito
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the KAITO pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kaito
``` 
Ensure that your cluster has enough GPU resources available.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the KAITO pods are in CrashLoopBackOff, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kaito
``` 
Look for configuration errors or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot reach the KAITO service, verify the service configuration:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace kaito
``` 
Ensure that the service is correctly set up and that the correct ports are exposed.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KAITO will result in the KAITO operator running in the 'kaito' namespace with all necessary pods in the Running state. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are healthy.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
